description_long: This marble sculpture depicts a dramatic scene with three human figures entangled by large serpents. The central figure is a muscular, bearded man with an expressive face, raising one arm while struggling against the serpents that coil around his body and legs. Two smaller male figures, likely his sons, are positioned on either side, also ensnared by the serpents and contorted in anguish. The composition is highly dynamic, with intricate anatomical details, flowing drapery, and lifelike textures. The base is rectangular and solid, supporting the complex arrangement of figures and serpents. The overall appearance is classical and monumental, reminiscent of ancient Greco-Roman art.
description: A dramatic marble sculpture of three human figures entwined by serpents, featuring detailed anatomy and expressive poses on a solid rectangular base.
description_view_0: The front view shows the central bearded man in full, with both sons and the serpents clearly visible, highlighting the emotional intensity and dynamic composition.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the depth of the sculpture, showing the figures' profiles and the serpents winding around them from a narrow angle.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the muscular back of the central figure and the arrangement of the figures and serpents from behind, with less facial detail visible.
description_view_3: The right side view presents the rightmost son and the serpents in profile, with the central figure's torso and arm raised prominently.
attribute_subject_matter: mythological human figures entangled by serpents
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
